output:
(B).

Statement 1 creates pressure to reduce spending. Statement 3 identifies the main threat as losing people to better-paying competitors. A 20% cost cut in that environment will almost certainly hit compensation or headcount, directly worsening the risk the consultant themselves flagged. Statement 2 demands *heavy* R&D investment, which is hard to reconcile with a 20% cut unless the company has enormous waste elsewhere — something the report doesn't claim.

Option C is the classic hand-wave ("just cut somewhere else"), but a 20% reduction is substantial. Without specifying where cuts fall, the recommendations are in tension by default. A consistent report would have addressed these trade-offs explicitly.
